subject: Esri / ArcGIS Tapestry license acquisition
scope: Documentation only. No engine code, mapper specs, candidate data, era-context data, EIG files, or browser/dist/output/live-data files modified.

terminology.legacy_boundary_shims: Legacy implementation shims are irrelevant to this procurement decision.

decision_summary: Defer Esri Tapestry purchase. PRISM simulator's blocking constraints for Phase 1 and Phase 2 are upstream of geography (mapper coverage gaps, candidate signature audits, vote-formula validation). For those phases, ACS + IPUMS PUMS + NHGIS is sufficient and reproducible. Esri becomes worth re-evaluating only at Phase 4, and only if state/county projections show geographically patterned residuals that ACS covariates fail to explain.

phase_1_single_year_mvp.rationale: Phase 1 is per-respondent: blocking constraints are mapper coverage gaps (AES absent, COM near-absent, ONT_H near-absent, PRO weak), candidate signature accuracy, and vote-formula sensitivity to high-uncertainty mapper outputs. Esri contributes nothing to any of these.

phase_2_modern_era_backtest.rationale: Same constraints as Phase 1 across 2008-2024. Worse: Esri current vintage is 2024+2025 only, so 10-15-year temporal mismatch on pre-2020 cycles. Effectively unavailable for the bulk of Phase 2.
phase_2_modern_era_backtest.alternative_for_poststratification: ACS 5-year per cycle + IPUMS PUMS for cross-year harmonization + NHGIS for boundary-consistent tract geography

phase_4_geography_projection.rationale: First attempt should use ACS-derived covariates only. Esri becomes a candidate covariate only if residuals cluster geographically and survive an ACS-only MRP fit.
phase_4_geography_projection.first_attempt_stack: ACS 5-year + IPUMS PUMS + NHGIS + county-level vote returns
phase_4_geography_projection.esri_role_if_revisited: MRP covariate or narrative coloring; not a primary political signal

lifestyle_segment_narrative_labels.esri_marginal: low — useful for reports, not models

what_esri_adds_beyond_acs.net_assessment: Esri adds consumer-behavior + media-consumption variance ACS doesn't capture. Empirical literature suggests neighborhood lifestyle covariates add 1-3% improvement in subgroup RMSE in MRP — small but real. For PRISM, this would manifest most strongly in Phase 4 county-level projections, not Phase 1/2.

what_esri_does_not_solve: Mapper coverage gaps (AES, COM, ONT_H, PRO are missing/weak in survey data)
Moral-boundary salience inference (per-boundary loadings derived from individual-level survey responses)
Engagement scalar inference (per-respondent from survey items)
Vote-formula validation (engine logic invariant to geographic enrichment)

no_political_attitude_content.detail: Tapestry segments correlate with political behavior but don't measure it. Risk: downstream consumers treat 'tract is segment X, segment X tends Republican' as direct political signal — correlation-as-causation. Violates design choice of keeping PRISM signature inference at individual level from survey responses.

paid_closed_licensing.detail: ArcGIS Business Analyst or GeoEnrichment credits required. Reproducibility constrained: anyone replicating the analysis needs the same license tier. Pricing is quote-based / plan-based / credit-based and varies by institutional posture; verify current pricing directly with Esri before procurement. This memo does not anchor to a numeric range.

current_vintage_mismatch_pre_2020.detail: Current Tapestry products ship 2024 + 2025 vintages only. Pre-2020 historical Tapestry not generally available. Using 2024-vintage segments to characterize 2010-era tracts introduces gross temporal mismatch (gentrified urban tracts, exurban developments). Effectively unavailable for 2008/2012/2016 backtests.

vocabulary_discontinuity_2024_2025.detail: June 2025 ArcGIS Tapestry replaced legacy Esri Tapestry Segmentation. Legacy 2024 (67 distinct + 1 unclassified segments / 14 + 1 LifeMode) retires June 2026. Current 2025 (60 distinct + 1 unclassified / 12 + 1 LifeMode A-L) is fresh release. 33 segments persist by name with potentially shifted footprints; 27 are new. NO official 2024 to 2025 crosswalk exists.

acs_derivative_circularity.detail: ArcGIS Tapestry built from Census 2020 + ACS 2019-2023 + Esri Updated Demographics 2025. If pipeline already uses ACS for poststratification, layering Tapestry on top is partial double-counting on the demographic axis. Defensible additive content (consumer behavior, media consumption) is a fraction of total Tapestry feature set; rest is re-packaged ACS.

acs_only.description: Census API directly via tidycensus or cenpy. ACS 5-year tables for matching cycle of each backtest year.
acs_only.verdict: Sufficient baseline for Phase 1, Phase 2, and first attempt at Phase 4. Esri marginal contribution should be measured against this.

acs_plus_pums_ipums_nhgis.description: ACS + IPUMS USA harmonized PUMS for cross-year individual-level demographic records + NHGIS for boundary-consistent tract/block-group geography across decennial reboundaring.
acs_plus_pums_ipums_nhgis.verdict: Recommended addition for Phase 2 cross-year work and Phase 4 geography. Same data lineage as ACS, no new vendor relationship.

esri_tapestry.description: ArcGIS Business Analyst or GeoEnrichment service credits for current-vintage Tapestry segments.
esri_tapestry.cost: quote / plan / credits — verify with Esri before procurement
esri_tapestry.verdict: Defer. Re-evaluate after Phase 4 produces a geographic residual map that ACS covariates fail to explain.

voter_files_l2_catalist_targetsmart.description: Per-individual voter records with vote history, party registration, demographic appends, contact data. ~210M-256M records.
voter_files_l2_catalist_targetsmart.verdict: Defer alongside Esri. Solves a different problem (individual-level turnout/contactability). If Phase 4 expands to per-state Electoral College forecasting with subgroup turnout modeling, voter files become more valuable than Esri at that point.

comparison_matrix.esri: quote / plan / credits — verify with Esri before procurement

recommendation.headline: Defer Esri Tapestry purchase
recommendation.phase_1_stack: ACS 5-year (matching cycle) via Census API (tidycensus / cenpy). Free, reproducible, sufficient.
recommendation.phase_2_stack: ACS 5-year per cycle + IPUMS PUMS + NHGIS for cross-year boundary consistency. Still free and reproducible.
recommendation.phase_4_first_pass: Same ACS + PUMS/IPUMS/NHGIS stack + county-level vote returns for validation. Attempt state/county MRP using ACS-derived covariates only.
recommendation.phase_4_esri_revisit: Only if ALL four revisit criteria are met (see revisit_criteria below).
recommendation.voter_files: Same defer/revisit posture. Re-evaluate only if Phase 4 expands to per-state Electoral College forecasting with explicit subgroup turnout modeling — separate decision from Esri.

all_four_required.detail: Weighted popular vote within ±3-5pp and correct major-party ordering for the modern era, per success metrics in 00-PLAN.md.
Residuals cluster geographically (suburban rings, exurban Sun Belt, Appalachian counties, etc.) rather than scattering randomly. Random-residual error is not a Tapestry-addressable problem.

Residual pattern survives a proper MRP fit using ACS demographic cells (race x age x education x income x density) at tract/block-group resolution. Means actually running the MRP with ACS covariates and inspecting the geographic residual map; not assuming Esri is needed before checking.

Esri license at project's tier permits storing derived segment-by-geography lookups internally for reproducibility of past analyses. If license-encumbered derivations cannot be persisted, reproducibility cost too high regardless of model gain.

partial_match_guidance.1_2_3_hold_but_4_fails: Consider open-data alternatives (county-level Census of Agriculture for rural cells, USDA urban-rural classification, NCHS density tiers, public Pew typology data) before paid commercial enrichment.
partial_match_guidance.1_and_2_hold_but_3_untested: Do NOT acquire. Test ACS-only MRP first.
partial_match_guidance.2_holds_but_1_fails: Geographic residuals are not the bottleneck. Focus on national-backtest blockers (mapper coverage, candidate signatures, vote formula).

official_references.note: For verification of current pricing, deprecation timelines, and segment vocabulary at the time of any future re-evaluation. Pricing on the buy page is dynamic / quote-based; do not treat any number cited in this memo as authoritative.
